diagrams.title: C Tokens Classification
diagrams.description: A hierarchical diagram showing the five main categories of tokens in C programming: Identifiers, Keywords, Constants, Operators, and Special Symbols. Each category is displayed with examples to illustrate what constitutes valid tokens in C.
diagrams.purpose: Explain that tokens are the smallest meaningful units in a C program, and help students recognize different token types when reading code.

diagrams.title: C Primary Data Types
diagrams.description: A visual representation of the four fundamental C data types showing their keyword, size in bytes (typical 32-bit system), and sample values. The diagram displays int, float, char, and double with their memory representation and value ranges.
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrate the basic data types available in C, their storage requirements, and the kinds of data each type can hold.

diagrams.title: Variable Declaration and Memory Model
diagrams.description: A diagram showing the process of declaring and initializing variables in C. It displays the syntax pattern (type name = value), visualizes how variables are stored in memory with addresses, and demonstrates the difference between declaration and initialization.
diagrams.purpose: Help students understand how variables are created, stored in memory, and the relationship between a variable's name, type, and the data it holds.
